terms.definition: A number that shows a part of a whole. It has a top number and a bottom number separated by a line. For example, 1/2 means one part out of two equal parts.
terms.exampleUsage: If you eat 2 slices of a pizza cut into 6 slices, you ate the fraction 2/6.

terms.definition: The top number in a fraction. It tells how many parts you have or are talking about.
terms.exampleUsage: In the fraction 3/4, the numerator is 3.

terms.definition: The bottom number in a fraction. It tells the total number of equal parts the whole is divided into.
terms.exampleUsage: In the fraction 3/4, the denominator is 4, meaning the whole is cut into 4 equal pieces.

terms.definition: Two or more fractions that represent the same amount, even though they use different numbers. For example, 1/2 and 2/4 show the same amount.
terms.exampleUsage: 1/3 and 2/6 are equivalent fractions because they both show the same part of a whole.

terms.definition: To make a fraction simpler by dividing the top and bottom numbers by the same number. Also called reducing a fraction.
terms.exampleUsage: You can simplify 4/8 to 1/2 by dividing both numbers by 4.

terms.definition: The complete thing before it is divided into parts. A whole can be a shape, an object, or a group.
terms.exampleUsage: A full circle is a whole. If you cut it into 4 equal parts, each part is 1/4 of the whole.

terms.definition: Parts that are exactly the same size. Fractions only work when the whole is divided into equal parts.
terms.exampleUsage: A sandwich cut into 2 equal parts gives you two halves.

terms.definition: One of two equal parts of a whole. Written as the fraction 1/2.
terms.exampleUsage: If you share a chocolate bar equally with your friend, each of you gets half.

terms.definition: One of four equal parts of a whole. Written as the fraction 1/4. Also called a fourth.
terms.exampleUsage: A quarter of an hour is 15 minutes because 60 divided by 4 is 15.

terms.definition: Fractions that have the same denominator (bottom number). They are easy to add because the pieces are the same size.
terms.exampleUsage: 2/5 and 3/5 are like fractions because they both have 5 as the denominator.

terms.definition: A fraction with 1 as the numerator (top number). It represents one single equal part of a whole.
The line between the numerator and denominator in a fraction. It also means 'divided by'.
terms.exampleUsage: In 3/4, the fraction bar separates the 3 on top from the 4 on the bottom.
